note:
{
  "experiment_overview": "The experiment aims to prepare cross-linked sodium alginate microspheres using different metal ions (Zn, Ca, Cu, Fe) through electrospay technology. The goal is to investigate the effects of these metal ions on the microspheres' properties, particularly their ability to inhibit bacterial growth.",
  "procedures_observed": [
    "A scientist in a lab coat and gloves prepares a solution of sodium alginate (2% concentration).",
    "The solution is then electrospayed into a chamber to form microspheres.",
    "Different metal ions (Zn, Ca, Cu, Fe) are added to the alginate solution before electrospaying.",
    "Bacterial suspensions of Staphylococcus aureus and Escherichia coli are prepared and incubated at 37°C for 12 hours.",
    "Blood samples from BALB/c mice are obtained by ocular phlebotomy.",
    "Microspheres are tested for hemolysis percentage using distilled water as a control."
  ],
  "materials_and_subjects": [
    "Sodium alginate (2% solution)",
    "Metal ions (Zn, Ca, Cu, Fe)",
    "Bacterial suspensions (Staphylococcus aureus and Escherichia coli)",
    "BALB/c mouse blood",
    "Distilled water"
  ],
  "tools_and_setup": [
    "Electrospay machine",
    "Test tubes",
    "Incubator",
    "Ocular phlebotomy device"
  ],
  "quantitative_observations": [
    "Absorbance measurements of the supernatant and positive control",
    "Hemolysis percentage calculation",
    "Temperature setting of 37°C",
    "Incubation time of 12 hours"
  ],
  "key_transitions": [
    "Preparation of alginate solution",
